name: gmrepo

download_files.notes: Core per-sample metadata file. Includes demographics (sex, host_age, BMI, country), phenotype (disease MeSH ID + phenotype label), sequencing metadata, and QC flag. This is the primary join table.
Per-run taxon relative abundance at genus and species level. Joined to sample metadata via accession_id/run_id.
QC status per run (QCStatus 1=pass). Links run accession to tool used (Qiime/MetaPhlAn4) and results version.
Pre-aggregated abundance statistics (mean, median, SD) per taxon per disease MeSH ID. Useful for biomarker/summary ingestion.
One row per phenotype (MeSH ID). Run counts, QC pass/fail counts, species/genus richness per phenotype. Lookup table.
Project-level metadata (SRA project ID, description, population, related publications PMIDs).
MeSH term lookup table. Maps MeSH UIDs to term names and descriptions.
NCBI taxonomy hierarchy table. Maps ncbi_taxon_id to scientific_name, rank, parent, and superkingdom.
Full MySQL dump of the entire database. Authoritative source if TSV files are incomplete or inconsistent.

MISMATCH: Content-Length header reports 0 bytes; file appears to contain only a header row with no data rows. Co-occurrence data may be empty in current export. Fallback: retrieve via API or SQL dump.
MISMATCH: URL returns the SPA HTML page instead of a file — this filename no longer exists in v3. Phenotype linkage is embedded directly in sample_to_run_info.txt.gz (columns: disease, phenotype).

fields.name: run_id
fields.notes: SRA run accession — primary key for joining to species_abundance

fields.name: disease
fields.notes: MeSH Disease ID — phenotype identifier, links to mesh_data

fields.name: experiment_type
fields.notes: AMPLICON or METAGENOMIC — distinguishes 16S from WGS

abundance_file_fields.name: accession_id
abundance_file_fields.notes: SRA run accession — foreign key to sample_to_run_info.run_id

primary_key.notes: The _id for each BioThings document should be the SRA run accession (run_id). This joins sample metadata, QC status, and abundance profiles.

license.observed_site: CC BY-NC (Creative Commons Attribution Non-Commercial License)
restrictions: Commercial use requires separate permission from journals.permissions@oup.com

plugin_inputs.join_strategy: Build one document per run_id: merge sample_to_run_info (demographics/phenotype) + samples_loaded (QC) + species_abundance (abundance array, grouped by run). Embed abundance as a nested list [{ncbi_taxon_id, taxon_rank_level, relative_abundance}]. Use stats_by_phenotype and mesh_data as lookup enrichment.

api_crawl_details.notes: API is available but designed for query access, not bulk export. Bulk download is strongly preferred — the TSV files cover 118k samples in a single download vs. paginating ~300 phenotypes × N runs via API. API is documented for programmatic exploration only.

paper_vs_reality.observed: 118,865 rows in sample_to_run_info.txt.gz (100 fewer — likely header + rounding in paper abstract)
303 rows in stats_by_phenotype.txt.gz (includes 1 header row → 302 phenotypes)
samples_loaded.txt.gz has 108,176 rows (all runs); QC filtering via QCStatus=1 will yield the verified QC-passed subset. Exact count requires filtering locally.
URL returns SPA HTML — file does not exist in v3. Phenotype data is embedded in sample_to_run_info.txt.gz (columns: disease, phenotype). MISMATCH in documentation but data is accessible.
species_cooccurence.txt.gz has header only — no data rows. File is currently empty in the bulk export.

paper_vs_reality.claim: Species abundance at genus and species level
paper_vs_reality.observed: Confirmed — species_abundance.txt.gz has taxon_rank_level field with values 'genus' and 'species'.

risks: sample_to_disease_info.txt.gz does not exist in v3 — phenotype data must come from sample_to_run_info.txt.gz directly
species_cooccurence.txt.gz is empty (header only) — co-occurrence data unavailable via bulk download; use API or SQL dump if needed
species_abundance.txt.gz is 57.79 MB compressed — decompressed will be ~400-600 MB; parser must stream rather than load into memory
CC-BY-NC: downstream commercial BioThings API consumers may violate NC clause
~30% gender fill rate — sex field sparse across many samples
Ethnicity not a field in any file — demographic-KG alignment is partial
